briefdescription.para: \par \b Purpose\/ \: Extract attachments from .docx files: OLE objects from word/ folder, plus diagram extraction (each diagram paragraph becomes a separate .docx attachment via BeautifulSoup reconstruction).

detaileddescription.para: \par \b Scope\/ \: docx attachment extraction: ZIP traversal, diagram isolation via BeautifulSoup XML parsing, OLE stream handling via base class. \par \b Input\/ \: File path to .docx document; optional parameters dict. \par \b Output\/ \: List of AttachedFile objects (images, OLE objects from word/; reconstructed diagram .docx files). \par \b Links\/ \: [USES_API(9): AbstractOfficeAttachmentsExtractor._get_attachments, AbstractAttachmentsExtractor._content2attach_file] \par \b Links\/ \: [USES_API(8): BeautifulSoup (bs4), zipfile.ZipFile, hashlib] \par \b Links\/ \: [USES_API(7): dedoc.utils.parameter_utils.get_param_need_content_analysis] \par \b Links\/ \: [USES_API(6): BadFileFormatError (dedoc.common.exceptions)] \par \b Invariants\/ \:

listitem.para: extract ALWAYS returns a List[AttachedFile] (may be empty).
BadZipFile is caught and re-raised as BadFileFormatError with a descriptive message.
Diagram extraction is attempted first; word/ attachments are added second. \par \b Rationale\/ \:

detaileddescription.para: Q: Why extract diagrams as separate .docx files? A: Docx diagram paragraphs contain embedded drawing data. Reconstructing each diagram paragraph as its own .docx allows downstream parsers to handle each diagram independently, providing better document structure. Q: Why read word/document.xml first, falling back to word/document2.xml? A: Some docx variants (older templates) use document2.xml as the main body. The fallback ensures compatibility. \par \b Changes\/ \:
LAST_CHANGE: [v1.0.0 – Semantic markup + LDD logging added] \par \b Module Map\/ \:

CLASS 9[Extracts attachments from .docx files] => DocxAttachmentsExtractor METHOD 5[Constructor: registers recognized docx extensions/mimes] => init METHOD 9[Main extraction: diagrams + word/ attachments] => extract METHOD 8[Extracts diagram paragraphs as individual .docx files] => __extract_diagrams \par \b Use Cases\/ \:
